audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar daily, Sabbath, new-moon, Passover, and Weeks offering calendar, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for continual offerings, Sabbath additions, new-moon offerings, Passover/Unleavened Bread, firstfruits/Weeks, grain and drink measures, and calendar layering.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Command children of Israel and say to them: My offering, my food for my fire-offerings, my soothing scent, you shall keep to bring near to me at its appointed time.
You shall say to them: This is fire-offering you shall bring near to YHWH—two complete male lambs, sons of a year, each day, as continual burnt offering.
The one lamb you shall make in morning, and second lamb you shall make between evenings.
And one-tenth ephah fine flour as grain offering, mixed with fourth of a hin beaten oil.
It is continual burnt offering ordained at Mount Sinai for soothing scent, fire-offering to YHWH.
Its drink offering shall be fourth of a hin for one lamb; in holy place pour out strong drink as drink offering to YHWH.
The second lamb you shall make between evenings; like grain offering of morning and like its drink offering you shall make it, fire-offering of soothing scent to YHWH.
On Sabbath day, two complete male lambs, sons of a year, and two-tenths fine flour mixed with oil as grain offering, and its drink offering.
This is burnt offering of Sabbath on its Sabbath, besides continual burnt offering and its drink offering.
At beginnings of your months you shall bring near burnt offering to YHWH: two young bulls, one ram, seven complete male lambs, sons of a year;
and three-tenths fine flour mixed with oil as grain offering for each bull, two-tenths fine flour mixed with oil as grain offering for one ram,
and one-tenth fine flour mixed with oil as grain offering for each lamb, a burnt offering of soothing scent, fire-offering to YHWH.
Their drink offerings shall be half a hin of wine for a bull, third of a hin for ram, and fourth of a hin for lamb. This is burnt offering of month in its month throughout months of year.
One male goat as sin/purification offering to YHWH shall be made besides continual burnt offering and its drink offering.
In first month, on fourteenth day of month, is Passover to YHWH.
On fifteenth day of this month is festival; seven days unleavened bread shall be eaten.
On first day shall be holy convocation; no laborious work you shall do.
You shall bring near fire-offering, burnt offering to YHWH: two young bulls, one ram, and seven male lambs, sons of a year; complete they shall be for you.
Their grain offering shall be fine flour mixed with oil; three-tenths for a bull and two-tenths for ram you shall make.
One-tenth you shall make for each lamb of seven lambs,
and one male goat as sin/purification offering, to make atonement/covering for you.
Besides burnt offering of morning, which is for continual burnt offering, you shall make these.
Like these you shall make each day seven days, food of fire-offering, soothing scent to YHWH; besides continual burnt offering it shall be made, and its drink offering.
On seventh day holy convocation shall be for you; no laborious work you shall do.
On day of firstfruits, when you bring near new grain offering to YHWH in your Festival of Weeks, holy convocation shall be for you; no laborious work you shall do.
You shall bring near burnt offering for soothing scent to YHWH: two young bulls, one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year;
and their grain offering, fine flour mixed with oil, three-tenths for each bull, two-tenths for one ram,
one-tenth for each lamb of seven lambs,
and one male goat, to make atonement/covering for you.
Besides continual burnt offering and its grain offering you shall make them; complete they shall be for you, with their drink offerings.

translation_notes: The calendar begins with daily rhythm, then layers Sabbath, new moon, Passover/Unleavened Bread, and Weeks upon the continual offering.
Numbers 28-29 complements Leviticus 23 by specifying sanctuary offerings rather than replacing the festival calendar there.
Passover and Weeks remain Israel's covenantal festivals and living Jewish sacred times; the text must not be Christianized or superseded.
Repeated measures create accountable ritual order, not a hierarchy of spiritual worth.
Animal-sacrifice instructions are source-preserved ancient sanctuary law and are not commands for private or modern reenactment.
safety_note: Do not reenact animal sacrifice, shame people unable to observe ritual schedules, or use this calendar to erase Jewish practice. Receive the chapter as ancient Israelite sanctuary order structured by daily faithfulness, Sabbath, liberation memory, and firstfruits gratitude.
